responses.subject: Legacy hook migration is not actually handled
responses.summary: Implemented full legacy-hook migration in the CLI and mirrored it in /gdlc-update. cli/init.js now: (a) tracks legacy entries via a dedicated LEGACY_HOOK_FILES table + isLegacyHookEntry predicate; (b) mergeSettings strips legacy entries from settings.json regardless of --force (legacy hook is unambiguously wizard-owned, so it can be cleaned without touching user-customised hooks); (c) planOperations emits REMOVE_LEGACY ops for any .claude/hooks/instructions-loaded-check.sh on disk, and executeOperations applies them via fs.unlinkSync; (d) check() detects legacy artifacts (file or settings entry) and returns DRIFT with a helpful details message + non-zero exit. skills/gdlc-update/SKILL.md step-7 mirrors the same logic — checks for legacy basename in InstructionsLoaded entries AND legacy file on disk, removes both. CHANGELOG migration-notes paragraph rewritten to reflect that legacy removal happens regardless of --force.
evidence: cli/init.js LEGACY_HOOK_FILES + LEGACY_HOOK_MARKERS + isLegacyHookEntry predicate (search for these names)
cli/init.js mergeSettings: walk-and-splice removes legacy entries before checking for current entry presence
cli/init.js planOperations emits REMOVE_LEGACY ops + check() flags legacy as DRIFT
skills/gdlc-update/SKILL.md step-7 bullets 4 and 5 — settings entry removal + disk file removal
tests/test-cli.sh: 3 new regression tests — test_init_removes_legacy_hook_file, test_init_replaces_legacy_settings_entry, test_check_flags_legacy_hook_drift. test-cli.sh now passes 27/27 (was 24/24)

responses.subject: Empty-stub state is internally contradictory
responses.summary: Resolved the contradiction by making step-5 Part B explicitly tri-state instead of binary: absent → write stub, zero-byte (the CLI handoff state) → write stub, non-empty → preserve and skip. Step-0.5's `Empty stub → Continue` branch is now consistent with step-5: zero-byte GDLC.md is recognised as the expected `npx claude-gdlc-wizard init` handoff and gets populated, never skipped. Detection uses `[ ! -f GDLC.md ] || [ ! -s GDLC.md ]` (test -s is true only if the file exists AND is non-empty, so the negation covers both absent and empty-stub).
evidence: skills/gdlc-setup/SKILL.md step-5 Part B — three-row state table (Absent / Empty stub / Non-empty file) with explicit detection idiom
tests/test-skill-contracts.sh::test_setup_handles_zero_byte_gdlc_stub — passes; would FAIL against pre-fix prose (it checks for `[ ! -s GDLC.md ]` OR zero-byte mention in step-5 context)

responses.subject: Step-5.5 idempotency is shallow for non-canonical headers
responses.summary: Step-5.5 append rules now spell out a case-insensitive, whitespace-tolerant header check using the canonical regex `^##[[:space:]]+[Rr]elated[[:space:]]+[Pp]laybooks[[:space:]]*$` — rejects accidental matches inside prose (the `^...$` anchors), but accepts `## related playbooks`, `##  Related Playbooks`, and trailing-whitespace variants. Skill prose explicitly notes the 'duplicates the section every run' failure mode that a literal-string match would cause. Existing test (`test_setup_links_surrounding_playbooks`) regex relaxed from `## Related [Pp]laybooks` to `[Rr]elated[[:space:]]+[Pp]laybooks` to accommodate prose mentions of the section.
evidence: skills/gdlc-setup/SKILL.md step-5.5 Append rules — first bullet now specifies tolerant regex inline with bash example

tests/test-skill-contracts.sh::test_setup_step_5_5_idempotency_is_tolerant — passes; FAILS against pre-fix prose (it greps for the `[[:space:]]+` regex pattern OR an explicit 'case-insensitive + whitespace' callout)

responses.subject: CHANGELOG assertion counts are wrong
responses.summary: CHANGELOG entry corrected to reflect the actual final counts. Whole-suite total is now 115 (not 110), because Codex round 1 prompted 5 additional regression tests on top of the original v0.2.2 changeset: 3 in tests/test-cli.sh (legacy hook migration regressions for Finding 1, P1) and 2 in tests/test-skill-contracts.sh (Findings 2 and 3). Final breakdown: cli=27, hooks=13, install-script=18, plugin=20, skill-contracts=37, total=115. CHANGELOG `Added` section reframed as 'whole-suite total 102 → 115' with split between contract tests (10 new) and CLI tests (3 new).
evidence: CHANGELOG.md::[0.2.2]::Added — `13 new contract / CLI regression tests (whole-suite total 102 → 115)` with a sub-bullet for each suite
Live counts from `bash tests/*.sh`: cli=27, hooks=13, install-script=18, plugin=20, skill-contracts=37, total=115

responses.subject: Required `git log --follow` verification on the new hook path currently fails
responses.justification: This auto-resolves on commit. The Codex r1 review was run against the staged-but-uncommitted v0.2.2 working tree (the rename was staged, not committed yet). After the v0.2.2 commit lands, `git log --follow -- hooks/gdlc-instructions-loaded-check.sh` will surface the pre-rename commits because the rename detection (`git mv`, 100% similarity) preserves history at commit-time. No code change required; the `git log --follow` verification is a post-commit check, not a pre-commit one. Round-2 reviewer should re-run the command after the v0.2.2 commit is created to confirm.
evidence: git status shows the rename is still staged (not committed): `renamed: hooks/instructions-loaded-check.sh -> hooks/gdlc-instructions-loaded-check.sh`
Verification gate: round-2 reviewer should run `git log --follow --oneline -- hooks/gdlc-instructions-loaded-check.sh` AFTER the v0.2.2 commit lands

responses.subject: A stale legacy basename remains in a shipped hook comment
responses.summary: hooks/_find-gdlc-root.sh comment line 3 updated from `Sourced by gdlc-prompt-check.sh and instructions-loaded-check.sh.` to `Sourced by gdlc-prompt-check.sh and gdlc-instructions-loaded-check.sh.`.

summary: All 6 findings addressed. F1 (P1) fixed with full legacy migration code path + 3 regression tests. F2/F3 (P2) fixed in skill prose with explicit detection idioms + 2 regression tests. F4 (P2) CHANGELOG corrected with accurate 102 → 115 totals. F5 (P2) disputed-procedural — auto-resolves on commit. F6 (P2) one-line comment fix. Total new tests in v0.2.2: 13 (10 contract + 3 CLI). All 115 assertions across 5 suites pass.
next_action: Run targeted recheck — verify each FIXED finding against its certify condition; verify F5 disputed justification.
